title: Can't enter containers with --userns keep-id: OCI permission denied
body:
**Description**

On an Active Directory environment, podman fails to run a command with --userns keep-id.

Works as expected with a local user.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Current uid/gid configuration:
```

$ cat /etc/sub[gu]id
bellegarde-c:100000:65536
bellegarde-c:100000:65536

$ podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map
         0       3121          1
         1     100000      65536
```

User information:
```
$ id -u
3121
$ id -G
2513 7256 7272 7287 10083 10085 10086 10087 10088 10090 10091 10092 10093 10094 10095 10097 10098 13041 13042 13910 13931 13992 13993 14260 15543 16084 17674
```


**Describe the results you received:**

```
podman run  --rm -ti --userns keep-id ubuntu ls
Error: crun: make `/var/home/bellegarde-c/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/12282f5bcb01f2b76298bb9408da12a058a1b37f0d3c28b418a22e842ab2011a/merged` private: Permission denied: OCI permission denied
```

But running this manual uid mapping fixed my issue:
 `podman run  --rm -ti --uidmap=0:1:1 ubuntu true`

Then:
 ```
$ podman run  --rm -ti --userns keep-id ubuntu ls
bin  boot  dev	etc  home  lib	lib32  lib64  libx32  media  mnt  opt  proc  root  run	sbin  srv  sys	tmp  usr  var
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.1
API Version:  4.2.1
Go Version:   go1.19
Built:        Wed Sep  7 21:57:53 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: UID/GID mapping seems to be working properly. @giuseppe PTAL

I am not able to reproduce on vanilla Fedora.

Could you share the output of `cat /proc/self/mountinfo`?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Actually, I encounter the same issue on OpenSuse MicroOS

```
> podman run --userns keep-id --rm docker.io/alpine:latest uptime
Error: crun: make `/home/solene/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/56c39578e10e299da920dac2b435296fc9d264989b6ccb42778cab622c7fde1b/merged` private: Permission denied: OCI permission denied
```

without `--userns keep-id` it works fine, but it's a requirement for distrobox :/
```
> podman run --rm docker.io/alpine:latest uptime
 20:58:12 up 11 min,  0 users,  load average: 0.66, 0.52, 0.25
```

Someone reported a similar issue on the same system https://reddit.com/12a65bl
The problem can be solved by applying a chmod 701 to my $HOME instead of 700, but I can't explain why.

> The problem can be solved by applying a chmod 701 to my $HOME instead of 700, but I can't explain why.

because root in the container (mapped to another UID than your user UID/GID) cannot access that directory since it is accessible only to your user
